extra.reasoning:
abdominal_pain appears only in r2 (priority 100): IF palpitations AND vomiting AND stiff_neck AND vision_loss AND abdominal_pain THEN acuity := self_care. This high-priority rule downgrades acuity, breaking monotonicity.

Baseline (abdominal_pain=false), with palpitations=vomiting=stiff_neck=vision_loss=true and fever=focal_weakness=slurred_speech=false: r2 does not fire (needs abdominal_pain). Firing rules are r3 (stiff_neck->urgent, pri2), r4 (vision_loss->emergent, pri3), r5 (vomiting->emergent, pri3), r6 (palpitations->urgent, pri2). Highest priority is pri3 emergent => acuity = emergent.

After setting abdominal_pain=true (all else unchanged, the witness assignment): r2 now fires => self_care at priority 100, which beats every priority-3 rule. So acuity = self_care.

acuity drops from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest) when abdominal_pain is set true. This violates the monotonicity property that setting abdominal_pain true never lowers acuity.
